mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 8.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 8.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 96 signals a strengthening supply chain that reduces production costs and increases corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 96 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 700,000 signals a strengthening housing market that increases consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 700,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month decline in the CPI above 225 signals a dovish monetary policy and low inflation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with dovish monetary policy and low inflation are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial jobless claims above 700,000 and a 2-month increase in the unemployment rate above 8% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with weakening labor market are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 95 and a 2-month increase in the GDP above 13400 signals a strengthening supply chain and economy that increases corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with strengthening supply chain and economy are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 4000 and a 2-month increase in the housing starts above 700,000 signals a strengthening consumer spending and housing market that increases corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with strengthening consumer spending and housing market are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
